_README: Seed corpus for everos memory extraction tests. user_facts seeds user-track episodes/profiles. skill_demo repeats one reusable procedure (with REAL tool round-trips — assistant tool_calls + tool results) across sessions so everos's agent_case clustering yields an agent_skill; per the EverMe openclaw plugin, case/skill extraction only fires on trajectories that carry tool round-trips. expect_keywords drive the semantic (non-exact) assertions.

messages.content: A couple of things about how I like to work: I always use dark mode in every editor and terminal, it's easier on my eyes.
Got it — I'll assume dark mode is your default preference.
Also, our backend project authenticates with OAuth2, and the primary datastore is Postgres.
Noted: OAuth2 for auth, Postgres as the main datastore.
One more: Alice is the project lead, so route design decisions to her.
Understood — Alice leads the project and owns design decisions.

skill_demo.query: how do I rotate a deploy key safely

messages.content: Rotate the deploy key for our GitHub repo without breaking CI.
I'll add a new key first, verify CI, then revoke the old one.
Generated ./deploy_new and ./deploy_new.pub
Registering the new public key and switching the CI secret to it.
Deploy key added; CI secret updated; test pipeline passed.
Pipeline is green on the new key, so I'll revoke the old deploy key to finish the rotation. Always add-then-verify-then-revoke so CI never has a window without a valid key.
We need to rotate the deploy key on our GitLab mirror too.
Same safe rotation: new keypair, register, verify, then revoke.
Generated ./gl_new and ./gl_new.pub
Adding the new key and repointing the CI variable.
Deploy key registered; CI variable updated; pipeline passed.
New key verified, revoking the previous GitLab deploy key now to complete the rotation.
Rotate the deploy key for the staging server next.
Generating a fresh keypair and installing it alongside the existing one first.
Generated ./stg_new and ./stg_new.pub
Installing the new public key and running a test deploy with the new private key.
Test deploy succeeded on the new key.
Deploy works on the new key, so I'll remove the old staging deploy key. Keeping both keys valid until the new one is verified is what makes the rotation zero-downtime.
